heading: Registration of freight forwarders
label: 13903 Registration of freight forwarders
notes: Amendments2015—Subsec. (d). struck out par. (1) designation and heading before “A freight forwarder”.Pub. L. 114–942012—Subsec. (a). , substituted “determines that the person—” for “finds that the person is fit”, added par. (1), inserted par. (2) designation and “is fit” before “, willing”, and, in par. (2), struck out “and the Board” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 112–141, § 32916(a)(1)Subsec. (b). , added subsec. (b). Former subsec. (b) redesignated (d).Pub. L. 112–141, § 32916(a)(3)Subsec. (c). , added subsec. (c). Former subsec. (c) redesignated (e).Pub. L. 112–141, § 32916(a)(3), added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 112–141, § 32107(b)Subsec. (d). , amended subsec. (d) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The freight forwarder may provide transportation as the carrier itself only if the freight forwarder also has registered to provide transportation as a carrier under this chapter.”Pub. L. 112–141, § 32916(a)(4), redesignated subsec. (b) as (d).Pub. L. 112–141, § 32916(a)(2)Subsec. (e). , redesignated subsec. (c) as (e).Pub. L. 112–141, § 32916(a)(2)2008—Subsec. (a). amended subsec. (a) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 110–244“(1) .—The Secretary shall register a person to provide service subject to jurisdiction under subchapter III of chapter 135 as a freight forwarder of household goods if the Secretary finds that the person is fit, willing, and able to provide the service and to comply with this part and applicable regulations of the Secretary and the Board.Household goods“(2) .—The Secretary may register a person to provide service subject to jurisdiction under subchapter III of chapter 135 as a freight forwarder (other than a freight forwarder of household goods) if the Secretary finds that such registration is needed for the protection of shippers and that the person is fit, willing, and able to provide the service and to comply with this part and applicable regulations of the Secretary and Board.”Others2005—Subsec. (a). designated existing provisions as par. (1) and inserted heading, inserted “of household goods” after “freight forwarder”, and added par. (2).Pub. L. 109–59

children.text: has sufficient experience to qualify the person to act as a freight forwarder; and
is fit, willing, and able to provide the service and to comply with this part and applicable regulations of the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Duration
children.label: b .—Duration
children.text: A registration issued under subsection (a) shall only remain in effect while the freight forwarder is in compliance with section 13906(c).
has at least 3 years of relevant experience; or
provides the Secretary with satisfactory evidence of the individual’s knowledge of related rules, regulations, and industry practices.

children.heading: .—Registration as Motor Carrier Required
children.label: d .—Registration as Motor Carrier Required
children.text: A freight forwarder may not provide transportation as a motor carrier unless the freight forwarder has registered separately under this chapter to provide transportation as a motor carrier.

children.heading: .—Update of Registration
children.label: e .—Update of Registration
children.text: The Secretary shall require a freight forwarder to update its registration under this section not later than 30 days after a change in the freight forwarder’s address, other contact information, officers, process agent, or other essential information, as determined by the Secretary.
